faqs.question: What causes drain fly infestations in Manhasset homes?
faqs.answer: Drain flies breed in the organic buildup inside drains, pipes, and areas with standing water. In Manhasset, older homes with aging plumbing systems and the humid Long Island climate create ideal conditions for drain fly breeding. Rarely used basement drains, kitchen sinks, and bathroom fixtures are common breeding sites.

faqs.question: How long does drain fly treatment take in Manhasset?
faqs.answer: Most drain fly treatments in Manhasset take 1-2 hours depending on the size of your property and the severity of the infestation. Our technicians will inspect all potential breeding sites, treat affected drains, and provide preventative recommendations. You'll typically see significant reduction within 48-72 hours.

faqs.question: Is drain fly treatment safe for my family and pets?
faqs.answer: Yes, we use EPA-approved products and methods that are safe for your family and pets when applied by our trained professionals. We can also offer green treatment options for environmentally conscious Manhasset homeowners. All treatments comply with New York State safety regulations.

faqs.question: Do you offer drain fly prevention services in Manhasset?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! Along with elimination, we provide ongoing prevention plans including drain maintenance treatments, moisture control recommendations, and regular inspections. Many Manhasset clients opt for quarterly maintenance to prevent drain fly reoccurrence, especially in properties with multiple bathrooms or basement areas.
